bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no opponent history yet and no time pressure to concede. I therefore choose the top value on each issue, especially holding firm on issueC and issueD because they carry the highest importance in my preferences.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the very beginning of the negotiation, and the opponent’s only offer is far below my preferences. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid, I make a tiny concession on low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issueC and other high-value terms.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer to me without conceding. I will avoid repeating my exact previous bid, but I will only concede on the relatively low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issues, especially issueC and issueD.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.06), and the opponent has only made a small concession by moving issueD to my preferred valueC while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC. I will preserve my most important issueC and issueD positions, while making a small change on the lower-weight issueB to avoid repeating my previous bid and signal limited flexibility.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.08), but I am making a visible concession on low-weight issueA. The opponent has held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC while conceding issueD to my preferred valueC, so I will hold strongly on the high-value issueC and issueD while offering movement where it costs me less.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on lower-weight issues. The opponent has consistently pushed issueA and issueB to valueA and has conceded somewhat on issueD, but they remain unfavorable on my most important issueC, so I will hold issueC at valueC and issueD at valueC while conceding issueA/issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by holding firm on issueC, which is by far my most important issue, while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueB. Since it is still early at t=0.12, there is no need for a major concession, but I can move issueD to valueD because the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest there and it costs me relatively little. The opponent’s offers are mostly low-utility to me and not clearly improving, so I should not chase them too aggressively yet.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.14, so I should not concede on the most important issueC. The opponent has consistently pushed issueC away from my preferred value, but has recently improved issueD to valueC and issueA to valueB, so I will accept movement on lower-weight issueB while holding issueC and issueD strong.

I am targeting a strong utility by holding firm on the high-importance issues, especially issueC and issueD, while conceding on the lower-weight issueA and issueB. Since it is still early at t=0.16, I do not need to make deep concessions, but the opponent’s latest valid offer already conceded issueC and issueD to my preferred values, so matching that structure may encourage agreement. Their behavior suggests they strongly prefer issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA, so I am conceding those low-weight issues.
